description: Saṅkhāra has two related meanings in the Canon. (1) As the second nidāna in paticca samuppāda: volitional formations conditioned by avijjā — kamma-generating mental intentions that produce future rebirth. (2) As the fourth khandha (aggregate): all mental formations other than feeling and perception. In the tilakkhaṇa context, sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā / dukkhā — all conditioned things are impermanent and unsatisfactory.
